# Beudox HR Portal - Technical Context

## Architecture Overview

Beudox HR is a modern React-based frontend application built with TypeScript, designed as a comprehensive Human Resources Management System. The application follows a component-driven architecture with clear separation of concerns.

### Technology Stack
- **Frontend Framework**: React 18 with TypeScript for type safety
- **Build System**: Vite for fast development and optimized production builds
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS with shadcn/ui component library for consistent design
- **Routing**: React Router for client-side navigation
- **Backend Integration**: Supabase (PostgreSQL + Auth + Edge Functions)
- **State Management**: React hooks with Supabase real-time subscriptions
- **Testing**: Vitest framework with minimal current test coverage

## Key Architectural Patterns

### Component Organization
- **Atomic Design**: UI components range from basic atoms (buttons, inputs) to complex organisms (forms, tables)
- **Feature-based Structure**: Components grouped by domain (attendance/, employee-profile/, leave/, etc.)
- **Shared UI Library**: Common components in `src/components/ui/` following shadcn/ui patterns

### Data Flow
- **Supabase Integration**: Direct database queries using Supabase client
- **Real-time Updates**: Live data synchronization for collaborative features
- **Optimistic Updates**: Immediate UI feedback with server reconciliation
- **Error Handling**: Comprehensive error boundaries and user feedback

### Authentication & Authorization
- **JWT-based Auth**: Supabase handles authentication tokens
- **Role-based Access**: Permission checks throughout the application
- **Secure Routes**: Protected routes with authentication guards
- **Password Management**: Secure password reset and strength validation

## Key Files and Their Purposes

### Entry Points
- `src/main.tsx`: Application bootstrap, router setup, and root rendering
- `src/pages/Index.tsx`: Main dashboard/home page component
- `src/App.tsx`: Core application component with routing logic

### Core Components
- `src/components/layout/AppLayout.tsx`: Main layout wrapper with sidebar and header
- `src/components/layout/AppSidebar.tsx`: Navigation sidebar with role-based menu
- `src/components/BeudoxLogo.tsx`: Brand logo with variant support (default/sidebar)
- `src/components/MandatoryPasswordChange.tsx`: Password reset modal for new users
- `src/components/SearchableEmployeeSelect.tsx`: Employee selection dropdown with search
- `src/components/NavLink.tsx`: Enhanced navigation link component

### Complex Business Logic
- `src/components/attendance/AttendanceSummary.tsx`: Comprehensive attendance analytics
  - Calculates attendance rates, overtime, punctuality metrics
  - Identifies anomalies (absences, incomplete records, weekend work)
  - Aggregates data across employees and time periods

### Utility Libraries
- `src/lib/utils.ts`: Common utility functions (cn for class merging)
- `src/lib/role-access.ts`: Permission and role checking functions
- `src/lib/notifications.ts`: Notification sending and recipient logic
- `src/lib/attendance-format.ts`: Time and attendance data formatting
- `src/lib/leave-utils.ts`: Leave balance and calculation utilities
- `src/lib/review-schedule.ts`: Performance review scheduling logic

### Integrations
- `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts`: Supabase client configuration
- `src/integrations/supabase/types.ts`: TypeScript types for database schema

### Hooks
- `src/hooks/useAuth.ts`: Authentication state management
- `src/hooks/use-toast.ts`: Toast notification system
- `src/hooks/useSort.ts`: Table sorting functionality

## Database Schema Insights

Based on the application code, the system manages:

### Core Entities
- **Employees**: User profiles with roles, departments, contact info
- **Attendance Records**: Check-in/out times, working hours, overtime
- **Leave Requests**: Leave balances, approvals, types
- **Payroll**: Salary calculations, allowances, deductions
- **Evaluations**: Performance reviews and salary adjustments
- **Projects**: Team assignments and activity tracking
- **Settings**: Company configuration (departments, roles, policies)

### Key Relationships
- Employees belong to companies with role-based permissions
- Attendance linked to employees with company-scoped holidays
- Leave balances maintained per employee per leave type
- Payroll generated monthly with complex calculations
- Projects have team members with activity logs

## Business Logic Patterns

### Attendance Processing
- Automatic calculation of working hours from check-in/out times
- Overtime detection (regular vs holiday/weekend)
- Late arrival identification based on company policies
- Absence tracking (explicit marks + implicit from missing records)

### Leave Management
- Balance tracking with automatic adjustments
- Approval workflows with notifications
- Working day calculations excluding weekends/holidays

### Payroll Generation
- Complex calculations involving base salary, allowances, overtime
- Tax deductions and final payout computations
- PDF generation for payslips and invoices

### Notification System
- Automated alerts for HR events (approvals, reviews, etc.)
- Role-based recipient targeting
- Email and in-app notification delivery

## Configuration and Environment

### Build Configuration
- `vite.config.ts`: Vite build settings with React plugin
- `tsconfig.json`: TypeScript configuration for the application
- `tailwind.config.ts`: Tailwind CSS customization
- `eslint.config.js`: Code linting rules

### Environment Variables
- Supabase project credentials (URL, keys, project ID)
- Configured for different environments (development/production)

## Development Workflow

### Component Development
- TypeScript-first approach with strict type checking
- shadcn/ui components for consistent design
- Custom hooks for shared logic
- Error boundaries for robust error handling

### Data Fetching
- Supabase client for direct database operations
- Real-time subscriptions for live updates
- Optimistic updates for better UX
- Comprehensive error handling with user feedback

### Testing Strategy
- Unit tests for utility functions
- Component testing with React Testing Library
- E2E testing with Playwright (configured but minimal)

## Security Considerations

- JWT token management with automatic refresh
- Role-based permissions throughout the UI
- Secure password policies with strength validation
- Input sanitization and validation
- Audit logging for sensitive operations
- Secure API communication via Supabase

## Performance Optimizations

- Component lazy loading where appropriate
- Efficient data fetching with proper indexing assumptions
- Real-time subscriptions for collaborative features
- Optimized re-renders with React.memo and useMemo
- Bundle splitting with Vite

## Future Considerations

- Expand test coverage for critical business logic
- Implement more advanced caching strategies
- Add offline support for critical features
- Enhance accessibility compliance
- Implement advanced analytics and reporting
- Add multi-tenant architecture support
